purpose: Evaluate feature flags against a normalized context so applications can enable, disable, or variant-route behavior without embedding flag logic in every caller.

endpoints.description: Evaluate a named feature flag against context attributes and optional default values.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Evaluate runtime feature flags against contextual attributes and policy rules.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use when a caller needs a deterministic feature decision based on user, tenant, environment, or rollout context.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use as a deployment system, experimentation analytics store, or remote config synchronization daemon.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Build the evaluation context. 2) Call /feature-flags/evaluate with a flag key. 3) Route behavior using the returned value and reason.

failure_modes.condition: Flag key is unknown or context is missing required targeting attributes
failure_modes.symptom: Fallback to default value with an explanatory reason
failure_modes.recovery: Define the flag or provide the required context fields before relying on the result

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Represent flags as typed rules with defaults, evaluate against a normalized context object, and return explicit reasons such as default, rule_match, or missing_context.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Flag evaluators become hard to trust when missing context silently produces truthy values; make defaulting behavior explicit and testable.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Keep evaluation pure and side-effect free so the same module works in APIs, workers, and local tests.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not mix metrics emission, rollout editing, or remote fetch loops into the evaluator.

test_cases.name: unknown flag falls back to default
